claim: Using AI agents directly as tools to main agent causes silent failures (partial task completion without error reporting), lost input validation, and hallucination amplification
actionable: Use sub-workflows (not direct agent tools) for multi-agent orchestration; add input validation, error handling, and post-processing in workflow layer
source_title: STOP building AI Agent Swarms in n8n (They're Broken) | n8n AI Agent Swarm Issues

claim: With sub-workflows as tools, you can control inputs (validation before calling agent), provide custom error messages for agent to align, and optimize costs by avoiding unnecessary LLM calls
actionable: Add validation nodes and if-conditions before calling sub-agent workflows; catch invalid inputs and return structured errors instead of passing malformed data to agent
source_title: STOP building AI Agent Swarms in n8n (They're Broken) | n8n AI Agent Swarm Issues

claim: Dynamic prompts from main agent forwarded to sub-agent (without structuring) increases hallucination risk; sub-workflows allow formatting inputs and controlling what data is passed
actionable: Structure all inputs to sub-agents in fixed formats (name, email, context, history); don't let main agent generate free-form strings for sub-agent consumption
source_title: STOP building AI Agent Swarms in n8n (They're Broken) | n8n AI Agent Swarm Issues

claim: Testing sub-agents in isolation (by running workflows independently) is easier than testing agents nested inside main agent (requires running full flow each iteration)
actionable: Design agents so sub-agents can be tested independently via workflow execution; set up sample inputs and iterate on prompts without touching main agent
source_title: STOP building AI Agent Swarms in n8n (They're Broken) | n8n AI Agent Swarm Issues

claim: Sub-workflow architectures enable feature expansion (add post-processing, notifications, logging) without increasing main agent prompt complexity; direct agent tools require more prompt engineering
actionable: Use sub-workflows to keep main agent prompt simple; all monitoring, logging, notifications happen in sub-workflow layer without complicating main agent
source_title: STOP building AI Agent Swarms in n8n (They're Broken) | n8n AI Agent Swarm Issues

claim: Sub-workflows enable reusability; single sub-workflow can be connected to multiple parent workflows, while direct agent tools are coupled and require duplication
actionable: Build sub-workflows as standalone units with clear input/output contracts; reuse across multiple parent workflows rather than embedding agents directly
source_title: STOP building AI Agent Swarms in n8n (They're Broken) | n8n AI Agent Swarm Issues
